payload.text:
PROPOSAL

- *Admin Module*: Admins will log in to update the status of electricity bills for multiple properties. They can view a list of properties, select a property, and change the bill status to "paid" or "unpaid".
- *Owner Module*: Owners will log in to view the status of their electricity bills. They can see which bills are paid and which are pending.

*Features*

- *Admin Dashboard*:
    - View list of properties
    - Update bill status (paid/unpaid)
    - View bill history
- *Owner Dashboard*:
    - View bill status (paid/unpaid)
    - View bill history

*Technical Requirements*

- Frontend: React  (web app)
- Backend: Node je (database)


Thanks

**Conceptualizing the React App**

I'm now focusing on the React application's structure. The Admin and Owner modules necessitate distinct component hierarchies, impacting routing and data flow. I'm considering using Redux or Context API for state management, crucial for handling bill statuses and property data. Thinking of specific API endpoints needed.


**Developing Functional Components**

I've outlined crucial functions: list, update status, and view history. I'm building out the basic file structure with components for the admin and owner dashboards, login, and mock data services. Mock data will simulate database interactions, and I'm considering integrating Gemini API for generating reports or offering insights as an AI assistant to the Admin. The initial focus is on functional components.


**Incorporating Gemini AI**

I'm now incorporating Gemini into both the Admin and Owner modules. The Admin will have an "AI Billing Report Generator" to summarize payment statuses, while the Owner gets an "Energy Advisor" for bill analysis and tips. I've also finalized the file structure for the app, focusing on core components for both dashboards and AI insights.  My goal is to keep the codebase concise. I'm keen to make the owner experience personal.


**Prioritizing UI/UX**

I'm now diving deep into the UI/UX for both the admin and owner dashboards. The goal is intuitive navigation and a clean, user-friendly interface. Thinking about the visual hierarchy of elements, and how to best display bill statuses and energy insights clearly. I'm prioritizing accessibility and responsiveness across different devices.

Below is a **much more detailed, enterprise-ready version** of your prompt with **extra rules, edge cases, security, UI behavior, and future-proofing**, written in **clear, professional language**.

You can use this directly for **AI code generation, developer handoff, proposal, or documentation**.

## 🔹 Enhanced Prompt: Role-Based Electricity Bill Management System

Design a **secure, scalable, role-based electricity bill management system** with **two primary user roles**: **Super Admin** and **Admin**.

The system must enforce **strict access control**, **time-restricted bill updates**, and **full auditability**.

---

## 1️⃣ User Roles & Access Control

### 🔑 Super Admin

The **Super Admin** acts as the system owner and has **global visibility**.

**Capabilities:**

* Create, update, and deactivate properties.
* View **all properties across the system**.
* Access **complete electricity billing history** for any property.
* View bill details **month-wise and year-wise**.
* View payment status:

  * `Paid`
  * `Unpaid`
* View timestamps and last modified user for each bill.
* Cannot modify bill payment status (read-only for financial integrity).
* Can assign properties to Admin users.
* Can activate or deactivate Admin accounts.

---

### 🔐 Admin

The **Admin** role is operational and restricted.

**Capabilities:**

* View **only assigned properties**.
* View electricity bills **month-wise** for assigned properties.
* Update bill payment status **only for the most recent 3 months**.
* Toggle bill status between:

  * `Paid`
  * `Unpaid`
* Cannot edit historical bills older than 3 months.
* Cannot add, delete, or reassign properties.

---

## 2️⃣ Electricity Bill Rules & Constraints

* Each property has **one electricity bill per month**.
* Bills are generated automatically at the **start of each month**.
* Bill statuses:

  * `Paid`
  * `Unpaid`
* Bills older than **3 months are locked**.
* Locked bills:

  * Are visible to all roles
  * Cannot be modified by Admin
* System should calculate **editable months dynamically** based on the current date.

---

## 3️⃣ Time-Based Edit Logic (Critical Rule)

Editable months logic:

```
Editable if:
bill_month >= (current_month - 2)
```

Example:

| Current Month | Editable Months |
| ------------- | --------------- |
| March 2026    | Jan, Feb, Mar   |
| July 2026     | May, Jun, Jul   |

All months before this range must be **read-only**.

---

## 4️⃣ UI / UX Behavior

### Admin UI

* Property list view with:

  * Property name
  * Current month bill status
* Month selector for bill history.
* Toggle switch:

  * Enabled → last 3 months
  * Disabled (greyed out) → older months
* Tooltip on disabled toggle:

  > “Editing locked for historical bills”

### Super Admin UI

* Dashboard with:

  * Total properties
  * Paid vs unpaid summary
* Property detail view:

  * Month-wise bill table
  * Read-only status labels
* Filters:

  * By property
  * By month
  * By payment status

---

## 5️⃣ Security & Validation

* Backend must enforce **role-based authorization** (not UI only).
* Admin requests to update locked bills must be rejected server-side.
* All status changes must:

  * Validate month range
  * Validate role permissions
* Prevent direct API misuse or date manipulation.

---

## 6️⃣ Audit & Logging (Highly Recommended)

* Log every bill status update:

  * Property ID
  * Month & year
  * Old status → New status
  * Updated by (Admin ID)
  * Timestamp
* Super Admin can view audit logs (read-only).
* Logs cannot be edited or deleted.

---

## 7️⃣ Notifications (Optional but Valuable)

* Notify Super Admin when:

  * An Admin marks a bill as `Paid`.
  * A bill remains unpaid beyond 3 months.
* Optional email / dashboard alerts.

---

## 8️⃣ Data Model (High-Level)

### Property

* id
* name
* location
* status (active / inactive)

### ElectricityBill

* id
* property_id
* month
* year
* amount
* status (Paid / Unpaid)
* last_updated_by
* updated_at

### User

* id
* role (Super Admin / Admin)
* assigned_properties[]

---

## 9️⃣ System Goals & Benefits

* Prevent unauthorized historical changes.
* Maintain accurate financial records.
* Clear accountability through audit logs.
* Simple operational flow for Admin.
* Full visibility for Super Admin.

---

## 10️⃣ Summary Statement

> This system provides a **controlled, role-based electricity bill management workflow** where **Super Admin maintains oversight and integrity**, while **Admins are empowered to manage recent bills efficiently**, without compromising historical data accuracy.

---
